user_query:
We're creating an internal troubleshooting runbook for recurrent issues our team sees during task installation, configuration, and a browser integration. Please read both input/incidents.csv and input/kb_seed.md, then produce two deliverables:

1) Write a triage report to output/triage/triage_report.json with one entry per incident ID (INC-001, INC-002, INC-003, INC-004). Use a JSON array where each object contains exactly these fields:
- id (string; one of: "INC-001", "INC-002", "INC-003", "INC-004")
- category (string; one of: "installation", "configuration", "tool-specific")
- probable_cause (string; concise, 1–2 sentences)
- recommended_actions (array of strings; ordered, actionable steps)
- severity (string; one of: "low", "medium", "high")
- prevention (string; 1–2 sentences)

2) Compile a human-friendly runbook to output/runbook/runbook.md that:
- Starts with an H1 title: "Troubleshooting Runbook"
- Includes a section titled "Problem-Solving Workflow" that outlines a 5-step workflow
- Includes a section titled "Experience Entries" with one sub-section per incident, labeled exactly as "### Incident INC-001", "### Incident INC-002", etc. Under each, fill this template with clear, concise content:
  Problem:
  Scenario:
  Solution:
  Root Cause:
  Prevention:
  Platform Notes:
- Includes a section titled "Quick Reference" formatted as bullet points where each bullet follows the pattern: "- Issue: <type>; Symptoms: <short>; Primary Action: <short>" (include at least 4 bullets)

Context: The four incidents to cover are: INC-001 (Windows global install error with an "EEXIST: file already exists" message), INC-002 (package installation fails with "404 Not Found" because of a likely wrong or unknown package name), INC-003 (a tool fails due to a missing environment variable), and INC-004 (a browser integration reports "tab not found" during follow-up operations). Use the experience summaries approach in input/kb_seed.md to categorize, propose fixes, and add prevention steps. Ensure both files are complete, consistent with each other, and written in clear, professional English.

Write only to the specified output/ paths. Do not create any other files.
